page_content: ARHU298L "Are you - Nobody - too?" Why we create and share stories, poems, comics and zines (3 Credits) Our ability to create and maintain meaningful social connections tells us that we are not alone; it reminds us that we are connected to something larger, a community, a group of people willing to celebrate our triumphs and help us cope during difﬁcult times. Emphasizing the principles of participation, connection, and collaboration, this course explores how various traditionally marginalized people form their own cultural communities and develop a sense of reciprocal belonging through the creation (and independent distribution) of stories, poems, spoken word poetry, comics and zines.In spite of intersectional obstacles such as the lack of political power, access to resources and economic opportunities, these communities may ﬁnd it possible to redeﬁne their place in society by demonstrating originality, resilience, and talent.
